title: LIC Golden Jubilee Scholarship
amount_description: Provides ₹40,000 per annum for Medicine, ₹30,000 per annum for Engineering, and ₹20,000 per annum for General Graduation, Diploma, or ITI courses.
benefits:
* Medicine courses: ₹40,000 per annum.
* Engineering courses: ₹30,000 per annum.
* General Graduation/Diploma/ITI: ₹20,000 per annum.
* Paid in installments directly via DBT.
docs_needed:
* Class 10 or 12 Marksheet proving >= 60%
* Income Certificate (< ₹4.5 Lakhs)
* College Bonafide and Admission fee receipt
* Aadhaar Card & Aadhaar-seeded Bank details
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **Access Portal:** Go to the LIC India official portal at [licindia.in](https://www.licindia.in) and locate the Golden Jubilee Scholarship link.
2. **Online Registration:** Fill out the online registration form. No offline applications are accepted.
3. **Course Details:** Select your current course stream (Medical, Engineering, Arts, Science, Commerce, ITI, or Diploma).
4. **Income/Mark verification:** Input Class 12 board percentage (must be >= 60%) and family annual income (must be <= ₹4.5 Lakhs).
5. **Submit:** Confirm and submit. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by their local LIC divisional office for document physical verification.
selection: Based on academic merit (minimum 60% in Class 12), family annual income under ₹4.5 Lakhs, and prioritizing candidates in lower income brackets.
renewal: Required annually by obtaining minimum passing marks (55% for professional courses, 50% for general streams) and maintaining regular attendance.
intro_seo: Complete guide to the LIC Golden Jubilee Scholarship. Learn about the ₹40,000 Medical, ₹30,000 Engineering, and ₹20,000 General Graduation grants, income limits, and how to apply.

title: Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship 2026
amount_description: The scholarship covers up to 80% of tuition or course fees, with the actual amount ranging from INR 10,000 to INR 1,00,000 depending on the academic level (e., INR 10,000–18,000 for Class 11–12, higher amounts for graduation/diploma). The funds are disbursed as a one-time grant covering tuition, books, and exam fees. The scholarship is not directly linked to Aadhaar-seeded DBT in the available sources; payment is made via the Buddy4Study platform to the student's institution or account as per the provider's standard process[1][2][4].
benefits:
* School (Class 11-12): up to ₹10,000 - ₹12,000.
* General UG: up to ₹15,000 - ₹20,000.
* Professional UG: up to ₹50,000 - ₹1,00,000.
docs_needed:
* Class 10/12/Previous class marksheet proving >= 60%
* Family Income Certificate (< ₹2.50 Lakhs)
* College/School bonafide & current fee receipts
* Aadhaar card & student bank passbook
step_guide:
# How to Register and Apply for Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship 2025-26

## Step 1: Log in to Buddy4Study
- Visit [Buddy4Study](https://www.buddy4study.com) and log in using your mobile number and password.
- If you are a new user, register using your email address and mobile number[4][6].

## Step 2: Open Application Page
- Search for "Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship Program 2025-26" and open the application page[4].
- Click on **"Start Application"** to begin[4].

## Step 3: Fill the Application Form
- Enter all personal, academic, and financial details carefully[4].
- Upload required documents: previous academic marksheet (showing ≥60%), family income certificate (≤INR 2.5L), enrollment proof, and ID document[3][5].

## Step 4: Verify Your Details
- Review all entered information in **Preview Mode**[4].

## Step 5: Accept Terms & Submit
- Accept the terms and conditions and submit the application[4].

## Track Status
- After submission, track your application status via the Buddy4Study portal dashboard[4].

**Note**: Deadline for 2025-26 cycle was 13-Mar-2026; new cycles open around September[2][4].
selection: Candidates are selected based on academic merit (minimum 60% in the previous qualifying examination), economic background (annual family income not exceeding INR 2.5 lakh), and enrollment in a recognized institution in India for Classes 11–12, undergraduate, diploma, polytechnic, or ITI courses. Applications are reviewed by Tata Capital Limited, and selected students receive financial aid plus educational and career mentorship[2][3][5].
renewal: The scholarship is not explicitly described as renewable annually. It is a one-time grant per academic cycle. Renewal or continuation is not guaranteed unless the student re-applies for the next academic year and meets the eligibility criteria again. The program operates in cycles with deadlines around March, June, and September[1][4][5].

title: PM Yashasvi Scholarship 2026
provider: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
amount_description: Provides annual scholarships up to ₹75,000 for Class 9 students, and up to ₹1,25,000 for Class 11 students enrolled in designated top-class schools.
benefits:
* Class 9 & 10: up to ₹75,000 per annum.
* Class 11 & 12: up to ₹1,25,000 per annum.
* Paid directly via DBT for school/hostel fees.
docs_needed:
* Caste Certificate (OBC/EBC/DNT)
* Family Income Certificate (< ₹2.50 Lakhs)
* Identity Proof / Aadhaar Card
* School bonafide showing studying in empanelled top schools
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **OTR Registration:** Register on the NSP portal at [scholarships.gov.in](https://scholarships.gov.in) to generate your OTR ID.
2. **Apply Fresh:** Log in using your OTR ID. Select "PM YASASVI Top Class School Scheme".
3. **MeeSeva/Income verify:** Input details of Caste (OBC/EBC/DNT) and Income certificate (< ₹2.50 Lakhs).
4. **Verify School Name:** Verify that your school is empanelled in the official YASASVI top-class school list.
5. **Submit:** Confirm details and submit. Disbursal goes directly to cover school fees via DBT.
selection: Selection is entirely merit-based, determined by the marks obtained in the qualifying examination of the previous class. The traditional YASASVI Entrance Test (YET) has been permanently discontinued. Priority is given to students belonging to OBC, EBC, and DNT categories with an annual family income below ₹2.50 Lakhs.
renewal: Required each academic year by maintaining passing marks in school exams.
intro_seo: Complete guide to the PM YASASVI Scholarship 2026 on NSP. Check OBC/EBC/DNT eligibility, list of empanelled schools, and online application guidelines.
faq_json: [{"question":"Who can apply for PM YASASVI?","answer":"Students belonging to Other Backward Classes (OBC), Economically Backward Classes (EBC), and De-notified Tribes (DNT) whose annual family income is under ₹2.50 Lakhs."},{"question":"Is there any entrance exam in 2026?","answer":"For the 2026 cycle, selection is based on school board marks and empanelled school enrollment criteria on the NSP portal (no separate entrance exam)."},{"question":"What is the scholarship amount?","answer":"Eligible Class 9 and 10 students receive up to ₹75,000/year; Class 11 and 12 students receive up to ₹1,25,000/year."}]

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (Karnataka)
provider: Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka
amount_description: Covers 100% compulsory tuition fees in government/aided colleges and provides maintenance allowances up to ₹1,200 per month.
benefits:
* 100% tuition and exam fee coverage.
* Monthly maintenance allowance of ₹250 to ₹1,200 (DBT).
docs_needed:
* Caste and Income Certificate (Digital SC)
* SATS ID / College Registration details
* College Admission and Nodal approved fee structure
* Aadhaar Card & Aadhaar-seeded Bank Passbook
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **Access SSP:** Open the portal at [ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in](https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in).
2. **Register/Login:** Register using SATS ID / student credentials.
3. **Select Scheme:** Click "Social Welfare Department" -> "Post-Matric Scholarship for SC".
4. **Upload Certificates:** Input Caste/Income RD numbers for auto-validation.
5. **Verify and Submit:** Confirm all fields and submit online. Follow up with your college clerk.
selection: Based on SC community status, Karnataka residency, and family income limit <= ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
renewal: Required each academic year through the SSP portal, uploading college passing transcripts.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, UG, PG, Professional courses)
amount_description: Provides full reimbursement of compulsory tuition, admission, and exam fees, alongside a monthly maintenance allowance. Disbursed directly to Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
benefits: Fee Reimbursement Category 1 (Income <= Rs 2.5 lakh): Complete reimbursement of tuition fees, admission fees, and examination fees. Full hostel fee coverage for eligible hosteller students. Maintenance Allowance Category 1: Rs 7,000 per year for day scholar students. Rs 13,500 per year for hosteller students in government hostels. Engineering/Medical Support Category 2 (Income Rs 2.5-10 lakh): Partial fee support for BE/B.Tech, B.Arch, ME/M.Tech, M.Arch, and Diploma in Polytechnic ONLY. Admission must be through Karnataka Examinations Authority - Common Entrance Test (KEA-CET) or Comed-K only. Amount varies based on course and institution. Direct Benefit Transfer: All amounts credited directly to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT. Additional Benefits: Support for both government and private institution students. Online process with no physical office visits required.
docs_needed: ST Caste Certificate, Income Certificate (annual family income <= Rs 2.5 lakh for Category 1, or between Rs 2.5-10 lakh for Category 2 engineering/medical), Previous year mark sheets (minimum 50% marks), Admission receipt/fee receipt, For Category 2: Proof of admission through KEA-CET or Comed-K, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate/proof of Karnataka residency, Academic records from UUCMS portal or university, NSP One Time Registration (OTR) ID - MANDATORY, For hostellers: Registration in State Hostel Portal (SHP) or SSP-HMIS portal
step_guide: Step 1: Complete NSP One Time Registration (MANDATORY FIRST STEP) - Visit scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal). Click 'One Time Registration (OTR)'. Complete Aadhaar-based eKYC and Face Authentication. Receive your OTR Number - REQUIRED for SSP application. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name matching Aadhaar, mobile number, email. Verify OTP. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Tribal Welfare Department - ST Category'. Enter NSP OTR ID. Choose category: Category 1 (income <= Rs 2.5L) for full benefits OR Category 2 (income Rs 2.5-10L) for engineering/medical partial support. Fill personal, academic, and family details. Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked). Answer domicile question (Karnataka resident? Yes). For Category 2: Indicate KEA-CET or Comed-K admission. For hostellers: Select 'Hosteller' only if in government hostel. Step 4: Upload Documents - Upload ST caste certificate. Upload income certificate (appropriate category). Upload mark sheets showing >= 50% marks. Upload admission receipt and fee details. For Category 2: Upload KEA-CET/Comed-K admission proof. Upload Aadhaar card and bank passbook. Step 5: Submit Application - Preview application. Verify all details. Check declaration box. Click 'Final Submit'. Download acknowledgment slip and note Application Reference Number. Step 6: Institutional Verification - Submit acknowledgment to college office. College principal completes e-attestation. Track verification status online. Step 7: Track Status - Log in regularly to check application status. Monitor for approval and payment updates.
selection: Selection is based on Scheduled Tribe (ST) category status, continuous enrollment in a post-matric course, and family income criteria (under ₹2.5 lakh for full benefits; between ₹2.5 lakh and ₹10 lakh for partial support in professional courses).
renewal: To renew, students must apply on the SSP portal using their existing credentials, show proof of passing the previous year's examinations, maintain required academic attendance, and have the institution verify their continuous enrollment.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students in Karnataka provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students pursuing education after Class 10. Offered by the Tribal Welfare Department through the State Scholarship Portal (SSP), this scholarship has two categories: full benefits for income up to Rs 2.5 lakh, and partial engineering/medical support for income between Rs 2.5-10 lakh. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents with minimum 50% marks in previous exam.

Step 3: Apply on SSP (SECOND APPLICATION) - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Create account or log in. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Minority Welfare Department'. Upload documents again on SSP (may require re-upload). Confirm domicile status (Karnataka resident? Yes). Step 4: E-Attestation (SSP Specific) - Visit SSP e-attestation portal separately. Upload documents for e-attestation. Visit your designated e-attestation officer IN PERSON with ORIGINAL documents. Officer will verify and e-attest your documents online. Note: E-attestation NOT required for PUC, ITI, GTTC, Diploma in Polytechnic/Education/Nursing/Pharmacy/Paramedical students. Step 5: Submit Hard Copies - Prepare TWO sets of complete application with all documents. Submit ONE full set to your college/institution principal. Keep ONE set for personal reference. Principal will forward application as per workflow. Step 6: Track Status - NSP Portal: Log in to check NSP application status. SSP Portal: Log in to check SSP application status and payment updates. Monitor both portals regularly. Address any document queries immediately.
selection: Selection is based on category eligibility (belonging to Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Parsi community), securing at least 50% in the previous final exam, and family annual income not exceeding ₹2,00,000.
renewal: Renewal candidates must apply on the SSP portal using their existing login/KUTUMBA ID, show passing of the previous academic year, maintain required attendance, and complete verification from their college.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to students from minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) pursuing education after Class 10. This scholarship has two components: PMS (Post-Matric Scholarship) for general courses with income limit Rs 2 lakh, and MCM (Merit-Cum-Means) for professional/technical courses with income limit Rs 2.5 lakh. Students must apply through BOTH National Scholarship Portal (NSP) first, then State Scholarship Portal (SSP). Karnataka domicile students studying outside the state are also eligible.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for EWS/General Category (Karnataka)
caste: General Category - Economically Weaker Section (EWS). Includes: Children of defense personnel (SC/ST parents in Army/Navy/Airforce, JCO/NCO/Lower ranks). Children of unorganized workers (auto drivers, daily wage laborers, construction workers, etc.). General category students with low income. Brahmin community students (separate scheme under Brahmin Development Board - verify).
amount_description: Provides a maintenance allowance ranging from ₹2,500 to ₹15,000 per year, and partial to full tuition fee reimbursement (up to ₹1 lakh for professional merit students under specific boards). Disbursed directly to Aadhaar-seeded accounts via DBT.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Partial to full tuition fee reimbursement depending on: Specific sub-category (defense/unorganized worker/general EWS/Brahmin). Department administering the scheme. Course type and level. Income bracket. Maintenance Allowance: Rs 2,500 to Rs 10,000 per year (estimated - varies significantly by scheme). Day scholar vs hosteller rates may differ. Defense Personnel Children Benefits (Special Category): For SC/ST children of defense personnel ONLY. Parents must be in Army, Navy, or Airforce. Rank: JCO (Junior Commissioned Officer), NCO (Non-Commissioned Officer), or Lower ranks. Higher income limit: Rs 2.5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh (unique provision). Partial fee support for technical/professional courses. Unorganized Workers' Children Benefits: Special priority in selection. Relaxed income certificate requirements. Covers children of: Auto drivers, Daily wage laborers, Construction workers, Other unorganized sector workers. Brahmin Development Board Scheme: Separate scheme for Brahmin community students. Income limit: Up to Rs 8 lakh per year. No income restriction for certain categories (verify on portal). Minimum 50% marks required. Direct Benefit Transfer: All scholarship amounts credited to Aadhaar-linked bank accounts. docs_needed: Income Certificate (range Rs 2.5-10 lakh depending on category), Mark sheets (minimum 50% marks), Admission receipt/fee receipt, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate/Karnataka residency proof, For Defense Category: SC/ST caste certificate of parent + Defense service certificate (Army/Navy/Airforce proof, rank proof - JCO/NCO/Lower), For Unorganized Workers: Parent occupation certificate/proof (auto driver license, labor card, construction worker registration, etc.), For Brahmin Category: Brahmin community certificate, Academic records from institution, Other documents as specified by specific department/scheme
step_guide: Step 1: Identify Your Sub-Category - Determine which EWS category you belong to: Children of SC/ST defense personnel? OR Children of unorganized workers (auto driver/laborer/construction)? OR General EWS with low income? OR Brahmin community student? This determines which department and scheme to apply under. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name, mobile, email. Verify OTP. Link Aadhaar with bank account. Step 3: Select Appropriate Department/Scheme - Log in to SSP portal. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select the appropriate department: For Defense: Department of Technical Education or Social Welfare (verify). For Unorganized Workers: Labour Department. For Brahmin: Karnataka State Brahmin Development Board. For General EWS: Social Welfare or other relevant department. Select the specific EWS sub-scheme matching your category. Step 4: Fill Application Form - Enter personal details (name, DOB, gender, contact). Fill academic information (course, institution, previous marks >= 50%). Provide family details (parent name, occupation, income). Enter income details (appropriate bracket for your category). For Defense: Enter parent's defense service details (force, rank, service number). For Unorganized Workers: Enter parent occupation specifics. Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked). Confirm Karnataka domicile status. Step 5: Upload Documents - Upload income certificate (appropriate range for category). Upload mark sheets (>= 50% marks). Upload admission receipt and fee details. Upload Aadhaar card and bank passbook. Upload category-specific documents: Defense: SC/ST certificate + Defense service certificate + Rank proof. Unorganized Workers: Occupation proof (auto license/labor card/etc.). Brahmin: Community certificate. Upload domicile certificate. Step 6: Submit Application - Preview application. Verify all details, especially category selection. Check declaration box. Click 'Final Submit'. Download acknowledgment slip and note Application ID. Step 8: Track Status - Log in regularly to check status. Monitor for approval and payment updates.
selection: Selection is based on General category candidates holding an Economically Weaker Section (EWS) certificate, with a family income ceiling (less than ₹8 lakh, e.g. for Brahmin Development Board programs), and a minimum of 50% marks in the previous qualifying exam.

intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for EWS/General Category students in Karnataka provides financial assistance to economically weaker students from general category who don't fall under SC/ST/OBC/Minority reservations. This includes special provisions for children of defense personnel (with higher income limit Rs 10 lakh), children of unorganized workers (auto drivers, laborers), and Brahmin community students through separate board. Income limits and benefits vary by specific sub-category and department. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents with minimum 50% marks.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities - UDID (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, UG, PG, Professional courses)
caste: All categories (SC/ST/OBC/Minority/General) - must possess valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) card issued by Department for Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Government of India.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Tuition fee coverage - amount varies by course type. Admission fee reimbursement. Examination fee support where applicable. Maintenance Allowance: Estimated Rs 2,500 to Rs 13,500 per year (exact amount not clearly documented). Varies based on: Disability type and severity. Disability percentage (fetched from UDID database). Course level (PUC vs UG vs PG vs Professional). Student type (day scholar vs hosteller - if hosteller provision exists). UDID System Integration: Disability data auto-populated from central database when UDID number entered. Eliminates need for separate disability verification in most cases. Disability percentage automatically fetched. Disability type automatically populated. Cross-Caste Support: Available to disabled students across ALL caste categories: SC students with disability can apply. ST students with disability can apply. OBC students with disability can apply. Minority students with disability can apply. General category students with disability can apply. Direct Benefit Transfer: All amounts credited to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Extended Deadline: Longer application window (December 31) compared to other categories, recognizing additional documentation needs for disabled students.
docs_needed: UDID (Unique Disability ID) Card - MANDATORY, Disability Certificate (if separate from UDID), Previous year mark sheets (minimum 50% marks), Income Certificate (family income <= Rs 2.5 lakh estimated), Admission receipt/fee receipt, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate/Karnataka residency proof, Caste certificate (if applicable for SC/ST/OBC/Minority), Academic records from institution, Medical certificate specifying disability type and percentage (may auto-populate from UDID database)
step_guide: Step 1: Obtain UDID Card (If Not Already Have) - Visit https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in (National UDID Portal). Click 'Enrolment for UDID Card'. Fill enrollment form with personal details and disability information. Upload disability certificate and medical documents. Submit for verification. Receive UDID number and card (may take time for processing). IMPORTANT: Complete this BEFORE SSP scholarship application if you don't have UDID. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name, mobile, email. Verify OTP. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens' OR 'Disability Welfare Department'. Fill personal details (name, DOB, gender, contact). Answer domicile question: 'Are you a resident of Karnataka?' - Select YES. Step 4: Enter UDID Information (CRITICAL STEP) - When prompted for disability information, select 'Yes' for 'Do you have disability?'. Enter your UDID number in the designated field. Click 'Get Data' or 'Fetch Details' button. System will automatically populate: Disability type (locomotor/visual/hearing/intellectual/etc.). Disability percentage (e.g., 40%, 60%, 80%). This data comes directly from central UDID database - verify it appears correctly. Step 5: Complete Application - Fill academic information (course, institution, previous exam marks >= 50%). Enter family details (parent names, occupation, annual income). Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked account). Upload required documents (see 'Documents Needed' section). IMPORTANT: For hosteller status, select 'Hosteller' ONLY if living in government-run hostel. Private hostel/PG accommodation = Day Scholar. Step 6: Submit Application - Preview application carefully. Verify UDID data auto-populated correctly. Verify all disability information is accurate. Check declaration checkbox. Click 'Final Submit'. Download acknowledgment slip and note Application Reference Number. Step 7: Institutional Verification - Submit acknowledgment slip to your college/school office. Submit UDID card copy and other required documents to institution. College principal/head will verify application online. Track verification status in your SSP login. Step 8: Track Application Status - Log in to SSP portal regularly. Check application status (Submitted/Under Verification/Approved/Rejected). If status shows 'Invalid Document', address issues immediately before deadline. Monitor payment status once approved.
selection: Selection based on: UDID Validation: System verifies UDID number with central database (swavlambancard.gov.in). UDID must be active and valid. Disability type and percentage cross-checked automatically. Students without valid UDID are ineligible. Eligibility Verification: Karnataka domicile status verified through certificates. Income verification (limit not clearly specified - likely <= Rs 2.5 lakh). Caste verification if applicable (SC/ST/OBC/Minority with disability). Academic Performance: Minimum 50% marks in previous examination mandatory. Academic progression and enrollment verified. Institutional Validation: Student enrollment in recognized Karnataka institution verified. College principal/head approval required. Academic records cross-checked. Document Verification: All uploaded documents validated. Aadhaar and bank account seeding verified. UDID card and disability certificate authenticity checked. Priority/Consideration: Differently-abled students across all caste categories eligible. Extended deadline (Dec 31) allows more time for disabled students to complete applications. Processing: Applications processed by Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka. Budget allocation for disability welfare determines approvals. Disbursement: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Payment timeline typically February-July after final approval.
renewal: Yes - renewable annually. Renewal conditions: Maintain minimum 50% marks each year. Continue meeting income eligibility. UDID card must remain valid. Fresh application required each academic year through SSP portal.
helpline: SSP Helpline: 1902. Email: [email protected]. Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka (for disability-specific queries). UDID Helpline: Check https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in for UDID card related queries.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to differently-abled students pursuing education after Class 10. Administered by the Department for the Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens through the State Scholarship Portal (SSP), this scholarship requires students to possess a valid UDID (Unique Disability ID) card. The system automatically fetches disability type and percentage from the central UDID database. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents with minimum 50% marks in previous exam.

title: Vidyasiri Food and Accommodation Scholarship for OBC Students (Karnataka)

step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **Access SSP:** Open the State Scholarship Portal Karnataka at [ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in](https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in).
2. **Log In:** Log in using your SATS ID / student credentials.
3. **Select Scheme:** Under Backward Classes Department, select "Vidyasiri Food and Accommodation Scheme".

selection: Based on OBC category (Cat-1 under ₹2.5L, others under ₹1.0L), Karnataka domicile, staying in a private PG/hostel, and study distance > 5km.

title: Merit-Cum-Means (MCM) Scholarship for Minorities - Professional and Technical Courses (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric Professional/Technical: UG and PG professional/technical courses ONLY. Admission must be through competitive examination.
caste: Minority Communities: Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi (Zoroastrian)

The disbursement is processed through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the student's Aadhaar-seeded, NPCI-mapped bank account.
benefits: Maintenance Allowance: Rs 20,000 per annum. Premier Institutes (85 listed): Full course fee reimbursement + Rs 20,000 maintenance (IITs, NITs, IIMs). Other Institutions: Rs 20,000 maintenance + partial fee support. Direct Benefit Transfer to Aadhaar-linked account. Central + State Support. selection: Selection is based on merit (securing minimum 50% marks in the previous qualifying exam and admission to professional/technical courses) and means (annual family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh).

intro_seo: The Merit-Cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities (MCM) in Karnataka provides financial assistance to minority community students pursuing professional and technical courses at UG and PG levels. Students admitted to 85 premier institutes (IITs, NITs, IIMs) receive full course fee reimbursement plus maintenance. Minimum 50% marks, family income below Rs 2.5 lakh, admission through competitive exam required. Separate from general Post-Matric Minorities scheme (PMS).

title: Pre-Matric Scholarship (Adi Dravidar & Tribal Welfare Dept.)
provider: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
amount_description: Provides fee concessions and maintenance allowance of ₹150 to ₹350 per month for SC/ST students studying in classes 1 to 10.
benefits:

docs_needed:

step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **School Registration:** Students do not need to apply individually on an online portal. The school administration initiates the process.
2. **Submit Certificates:** Submit your Community Certificate copy, Aadhaar card, and student bank passbook copy to your class teacher.
3. **Verify Income:** For classes 9 and 10, submit an Income Certificate indicating annual family income is under ₹2.5 Lakhs.
4. **Data Entry:** The school headmaster enters student details into the EMIS (Education Management Information System) database.
5. **Credit:** Approved scholarship funds are directly credited to the student's or parent's bank account.
selection: Based on SC/ST/SCA/SCC category, enrollment in classes 1-10 in government/aided schools, and meeting income limits (for classes 9-10).
renewal: Automatically renewed each academic year based on school promotion records entered in the EMIS portal.

title: Tamil Pudhalvan Scheme
provider: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu

### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **School Certificates:** Secure certificates verifying you studied in Tamil Nadu Government or Government-Aided Tamil Medium schools from Class 6 to Class 12.
2. **Nodal Officer Submission:** Bring your school records, Aadhaar card, and Aadhaar-seeded bank passbook copy to your college admission or scholarship desk.

title: Oasis Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (West Bengal)
provider: Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal
amount_description: Maintenance allowance: ₹150/month for day scholars, ₹350/month for hostellers (paid for 10 months). Ad-hoc grant: ₹750/year for day scholars, ₹1,000/year for hostellers.
benefits: Maintenance allowance of ₹1,500 to ₹3,500 per annum, plus an annual ad-hoc grant of ₹750 (day scholars) or ₹1,000 (hostellers) to cover school expenses and tuition fees.
docs_needed:
Aadhaar Card
Caste Certificate issued by WB Government
Previous Class Marksheet
Income Certificate
Bank Passbook showing IFSC and Account Number
Passport-size Photograph
School Bonafide Certificate
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **Visit Oasis Portal:** Go to the official OASIS website at [oasis.gov.in](https://oasis.gov.in).
2. **Student Registration:** Click on the "Student's Registration" link. Select the district where your school is located.
3. **Verify Caste Certificate:** Enter your computerised Caste Certificate number and issue date, then submit.
4. **Fill Basic Details:** Enter your school name, class, mobile number, email, and bank account details. Submit to generate your Application ID and Password.
5. **Login and Apply:** Log in using your credentials. Click "Apply Now" and fill out the detailed form (academic info, parent income details).
6. **Bank Verification:** Enter your bank account details (IFSC, Account Number) carefully. Ensure Aadhaar is linked.
7. **Verify & Lock:** Double-check all entered information. Click "Verify and Lock Application". Note: No changes can be made after locking.
8. **Print & Submit:** Download the PDF application form. Print it, attach the required documents (income cert, marksheet, caste cert, bank proof), and submit it to your school Principal for verification.
selection: Based on satisfying the eligibility criteria: West Bengal domicile, valid SC caste certificate, enrollment in Class 9 or 10, and annual family income below ₹2.5 Lakh.
renewal: Must reapply online each academic year through the Oasis portal using the renewal login option.
intro_seo: Apply for the Oasis Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students in West Bengal. Check official eligibility criteria, annual family income limits, required documents list, monthly stipends, and online step-by-step application guide for Class 9 and 10 SC day scholars and hostellers on oasis.gov.in.
faq_json: [{"question":"Who can apply for the Oasis Pre-Matric SC Scholarship?","answer":"Students belonging to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category who are permanent residents of West Bengal, studying in Class 9 or 10, and have an annual family income of less than ₹2.5 Lakh."},{"question":"Is a caste certificate from another state valid for this scholarship?","answer":"No. The caste certificate must be computerised and issued by the competent authority of the Government of West Bengal."},{"question":"How is the scholarship money disbursed?","answer":"The scholarship amount is transferred directly to the student's Aadhaar-seeded bank account through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode."}]

title: Oasis Pre-Matric Scholarship for ST Students (West Bengal)
provider: Tribal Development Department, Government of West Bengal
amount_description: Maintenance allowance: ₹150/month for day scholars, ₹350/month for hostellers (paid for 10 months). Ad-hoc grant: ₹750/year for day scholars, ₹1,000/year for hostellers.
benefits: Maintenance allowance of ₹1,500 to ₹3,500 per annum, plus an annual ad-hoc grant of ₹750 (day scholars) or ₹1,000 (hostellers) to cover school expenses and tuition fees.
docs_needed:
Aadhaar Card
ST Caste Certificate issued by WB Government
Previous Class Marksheet
Income Certificate
Bank Passbook showing IFSC and Account Number
Passport-size Photograph
School Bonafide Certificate
NSP OTR ID Registration Proof
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **NSP Registration (Mandatory for ST):** Go to the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in) and register to generate your One-Time Registration (OTR) ID.
2. **Visit Oasis Portal:** Go to the official OASIS website at [oasis.gov.in](https://oasis.gov.in).
3. **Student Registration:** Click on "Student's Registration" and select the district of your school.
4. **Enter Credentials & OTR:** Verify your West Bengal computerised ST Caste Certificate and enter your NSP OTR ID.
5. **Generate Account:** Fill out basic details (mobile, email, school, bank details) to receive your Application ID and Password.
6. **Login & Complete Form:** Log in on the Oasis portal, fill in all educational and parent details, and enter your bank IFSC/account details.

title: Kanyashree Prakalpa Scheme (K1 & K2) (West Bengal)
provider: Department of Women and Child Development and Social Welfare, Government of West Bengal
amount_description: K1 (Annual Stipend): ₹1,000/year for unmarried girls aged 13-18 (Class 8-12). K2 (One-Time Grant): ₹25,000 paid upon turning 18, provided the student remains unmarried and is enrolled in higher education or training.
benefits: Annual stipend of ₹1,000 for younger students (K1) and a major one-time developmental grant of ₹25,000 (K2) to promote girl child education, prevent early marriage, and support higher studies or vocational training.
age_limit: 13-18 years for K1; 18 years for K2
docs_needed:
Aadhaar Card
Birth Certificate
Unmarried Self-Declaration (signed by student and parent)
Previous Class Marksheet
Income Certificate (signed by competent authority)
Bank Passbook in the student's name
Passport-size Photograph
Bonafide Student Certificate from Institution
JJ Home Inmate Certificate or Disability Certificate (if applying for income limit waiver)
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **Obtain Form:** Collect the Kanyashree Application Form (Form K1 for annual scholarship, Form K2 for one-time grant) free of cost from your school or college.
2. **Fill Details:** Complete the form carefully with personal details, bank account information, and institutional details.
3. **Open Bank Account:** Ensure you have a bank account in your name. WBMDFC/School authorities often help arrange student bank accounts.
4. **Sign Declarations:** The student must sign the unmarried declaration. The parent/guardian must also sign the declaration of marital status and family income.
5. **Attach Documents:** Gather copies of your Aadhaar, birth certificate, bank passbook, previous marksheet, and passport photo.
6. **Submit to School:** Submit the filled form with attached documents directly to your school/college office.
7. **School Portal Upload:** The institution authorities will verify your documents and upload the data to the official Kanyashree Portal.
8. **Receive Acknowledgment:** You will receive a system-generated acknowledgment slip with your Kanyashree ID for tracking your status online.
selection: Direct benefit transfer upon satisfying all eligibility criteria (unmarried female, domicile of West Bengal, age limits, regular educational enrollment, and family income below ₹1.2 Lakh). The income limit is waived for orphans, JJ Home inmates, and students with >40% disability.
renewal: K1 is renewed annually through the school portal. K2 is a one-time grant upon turning 18 and requires a separate application.

title: MEXT Scholarship (Japan Government)
provider: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), Japan
amount_description: Fully funded: monthly stipend ¥148,000 (~₹83,000) for research students; ¥117,000 (~₹65,000) for UG. Covers tuition, accommodation, and one-time travel allowance to/from Japan.
intro_seo: The MEXT Scholarship (Japanese Government Scholarship) is a fully funded programme from Japan's Ministry of Education for international students including Indians. For Indian students, the primary route is the Embassy Recommendation track through the Embassy of Japan in New Delhi. MEXT covers 7 scholarship categories: Research Students (for Master's/PhD), Undergraduate, College of Technology, Specialized Training College, Teacher Training, Japanese Studies, and Young Leaders Programme. Competition has surged — Research Student applications jumped from ~3,170 to 7,300+ in one year, dropping acceptance to ~2.5%.

title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters (EMJM)
provider: European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA), European Commission
amount_description: Fully funded: monthly living allowance €1,000–1,400/month (~₹89,000–₹1.25L/month), tuition fee waiver, travel allowance, and visa/insurance costs. Exact amounts vary by programme consortium.

With 200+ active programmes across all disciplines, Erasmus Mundus is one of the most accessible fully funded options for Indian students going to Europe, particularly for those not targeting the UK or US.

title: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
amount_description: The scholarship is valued at $40,000 CAD per year for three years (non-renewable), totaling $120,000 CAD over the duration. This amount is intended to support tuition fees, living expenses, and research costs. Additional funding for research travel may be available through the Canada Research Training Awards Suite—Research Travel Supplement.
benefits:
- **Nationality:** Open to Canadian citizens, permanent residents of Canada, protected persons under subsection 95(2) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (Canada), and international students.
- **International Students:** Must be registered in a doctoral program of study at an eligible Canadian institution by the application deadline. Up to 15% of awards will be available to international applicants.
- **Degree Level:** Must be pursuing a first doctoral degree (PhD), including joint programs such as MD/PhD, DVM/PhD, JD/PhD, MA/PhD, or MSc/PhD, provided the program includes a significant autonomous research component.
- **Months of Study:** Must have completed no more than 36 months of full-time equivalent study in their doctoral program (or PhD portion of a joint program) by December 31 of the calendar year of application.
- **Academic Standing:** Must have achieved a first-class average (typically equivalent to an “A-” or “A” baseline, as defined by the specific host university) in each of their last two completed years of full-time study.
- **Previous Awards:** Cannot hold or have held a doctoral-level scholarship or fellowship from CIHR, NSERC, or SSHRC (including a Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarship) to undertake or complete a doctoral degree.
- **Application Limit:** Can apply a maximum of three times to the Canada Graduate Research Scholarship – Doctoral (CGRS D) program.
- **Faculty Appointment:** Cannot hold a faculty appointment concurrently with the CGRS D award unless they arrange for a leave of absence.
- **Nomination:** Applicants must generally be nominated by an eligible Canadian institution. Canadian citizens or permanent residents who completed a degree at a Canadian institution but are currently enrolled at a foreign university may apply directly to the federal agencies.
docs_needed:
ResearchNet application form (or NSERC/SSHRC online system equivalent)
Canadian Common CV (CCV)
Official, up-to-date transcripts of all undergraduate and graduate studies (completed and ongoing), including a copy of the legend/key. Must show current Fall term registration if applicable.
CGRS D Timeline for Doctoral Studies Form (to be included with transcripts)
Research project summary / Research proposal
Research Contributions (maximum one page)
Two sponsor assessments / Letters of reference/appraisal
Proof of citizenship (mandatory for all applicants)
CGRS D Confirmation of Scientific Area form
Training Expectations (for CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC, typically 2 pages)
Special Circumstances (optional, maximum 1 page)
Self-Declaration of No Institutional Affiliation with a Quota (optional, for direct applicants)
CGRS D Indigenous Attestation Form (optional, for Indigenous applicants)
step_guide:
The Canada Graduate Research Scholarship – Doctoral (CGRS D) application process is primarily institution-driven:

1.  **Identify a Nominating Institution:** Research Canadian universities with a CGRS D quota and identify the institution where you intend to pursue your doctoral studies.
2.  **Contact Graduate Faculty:** Inform the faculty of graduate studies at your chosen university of your intent to apply for the CGRS D. Universities often have internal screening processes and deadlines (which are significantly earlier than the federal deadlines, typically in August or September).
3.  **Complete Online Application:** Access and complete the CGRS D application form through the relevant Tri-Agency portal: ResearchNet (for CIHR), NSERC Online System (for NSERC), or SSHRC Web-based Forms portal (for SSHRC).
4.  **Prepare Documents:** Gather and prepare all required application materials, including your Canadian Common CV (CCV), official transcripts, research proposal, and arrange for two sponsors to submit their assessments.
5.  **Submit to Institution (or Directly to Agency):** Submit your complete application package to your nominating institution by their internal deadline. If you are a Canadian citizen or permanent resident who completed a Canadian degree but are studying abroad, you may be eligible to apply directly to the federal agencies (typically by mid-October).
6.  **University Review and Nomination:** The institution will review internal applications and forward selected nominations to the appropriate federal granting agency.
7.  **Agency Review and Notification:** The federal granting agencies will conduct a national review and evaluation of nominations. Anticipated notification of results is typically in April of the following year.
selection: Applications are evaluated based on two equally weighted selection criteria: Research Potential (50%) and Relevant Experience and Achievements obtained within and beyond academia (50%). The review process typically involves an initial internal review and ranking by the applicant's nominating university, followed by a national competition adjudicated by agency-specific selection committees (CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC).
renewal: The scholarship is non-renewable and is awarded for a fixed term of three years (36 months). Award holders must remain enrolled full-time in their doctoral program and demonstrate continued satisfactory academic progress to maintain the award. Post-award policies and regulations are governed by the Tri-Agency Research Training Award Holder's Guide.

title: Canada Graduate Scholarships – Master’s Program
amount_description: The scholarship provides an annual grant of $27,000 CAD for one year, non-renewable. This amount is intended to support students in concentrating fully on their studies. As of July 12, 2026, this is approximately 1,818,792 INR (based on an exchange rate of 1 CAD = 67.36 INR).
benefits:
- **Citizenship:** Must be a Canadian citizen, a permanent resident of Canada, or a Protected Person under subsection 95(2) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (Canada) as of the application deadline date.
- **Degree Level:** Must be enrolled in, have applied for, or will apply for full-time admission to an eligible graduate program at the master's or doctoral level that can be started within the eligible dates of the competition year at a Canadian institution with a CGRS M allocation.
- **Study Progress:** As of December 31 of the year of application, applicants must have completed between zero and 12 months of full-time studies (or full-time equivalent) in the master's program for which funding is requested. This also applies to direct-entry doctoral programs or master's programs transitioning to doctoral programs.
- **Academic Achievement:** Applicants must demonstrate a high standard of achievement in undergraduate and early graduate studies, typically requiring a first-class average (e.g., approximately 80% or an A-/3.6 GPA equivalent) in each of the last two full completed years of study.
- **Institution Application:** Incoming students are often required to submit an application for admission to their intended Canadian university/program by the CGRS-M application deadline to be considered for the award.
docs_needed:
Completed online application form via the Research Portal (including Identification, Summary of Proposal, and Activity Details sections)
Outline of proposed research (1 page)
Bibliography and citations (1 page)
Canadian Common CV (CCV) confirmation number
All up-to-date undergraduate and graduate transcripts (including transcript keys/legends)
Two reference assessments (completed by referees via an online form)
step_guide:
1. **Review Eligibility & Program Guidelines:** Carefully read the official CGRS M program description and instructions on the NSERC website to ensure you meet all eligibility criteria, including academic requirements and citizenship status.
2. **Select Granting Agency:** Determine which of the three federal granting agencies (CIHR, NSERC, or SSHRC) is most appropriate for your research field. Consult the 'Selecting the Appropriate Federal Granting Agency' guidelines if unsure.
3. **Apply for University Admission (if applicable):** If you are not currently enrolled in your intended graduate program, submit an application for admission to your chosen Canadian university/program by their internal deadline, which may coincide with the CGRS M application deadline.
4. **Create/Update Canadian Common CV (CCV):** Complete and validate your Canadian Common CV (CCV) for the correct funding agency. Obtain the confirmation number, which will be needed for your scholarship application.
5. **Access Research Portal:** Log in to the online Research Portal (or create an account if you don't have one). This is the single platform for submitting your CGRS M application.
6. **Complete Application Form:** Fill out all sections of the online application, including personal identification, summary of your research proposal, and activity details. Select up to three eligible Canadian institutions where you wish to hold the award.
7. **Prepare and Upload Documents:** Upload your proposed research outline (1 page) and bibliography (1 page) as PDFs. Ensure all up-to-date undergraduate and graduate transcripts (including the transcript key/legend) are uploaded.
8. **Request Reference Assessments:** Invite two referees through the Research Portal to complete and submit their reference assessments by an early deadline you communicate to them, as your application cannot be submitted until these are linked.
9. **Submit Application:** Ensure all sections are complete, documents are uploaded, and references are submitted. Submit your application through the Research Portal by the national deadline, typically December 1 (8:00 PM ET).
10. **Monitor Results:** Regularly check the Research Portal for your application status and award offers, typically from April 1 onwards.
11. **Accept/Decline Offer:** If offered an award, you will have 21 days to accept or decline it in the Research Portal.
selection:
The selection process for the Canada Graduate Research Scholarship – Master’s (CGRS M) is primarily merit-based and carried out at the university level under the guidance of the three granting agencies (CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC).. Applications are evaluated based on the following criteria:
- **Academic excellence (50%)**
- **Research potential of the candidate (30%)**
- **Skills and experiences (20%)**

Adjudication committees composed of faculty members review and rank nominated applications to select award recipients. Offers of awards are typically made available in the Research Portal around April 1.
renewal: The Canada Graduate Research Scholarship – Master’s (CGRS M) is a non-renewable award, typically for a duration of 12 months.

title: Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship Program
amount_description: The Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship that covers tuition fees, books, incidental fees, and full residence support for four years of undergraduate study. The total value for four years can exceed CAD $300,000, with annual tuition alone potentially over CAD $60,000, depending on the program. Based on an estimated annual value of CAD $75,000, this equates to approximately 5,055,000 INR annually (using an exchange rate of 1 CAD = 67.4 INR).
benefits:
- Must be an international student (i.e., a non-Canadian requiring a study permit).
- Must be currently enrolled in your final year of senior secondary school in 2026/2027 or have graduated no earlier than June 2026.
- Must be beginning your studies at the University of Toronto in September 2027.
- Students already attending post-secondary studies or starting post-secondary studies in January 2027 at the University of Toronto or another post-secondary institution are not eligible.
- Must be nominated by your high school.
- Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement, creativity, and leadership potential.
docs_needed:
High school nomination (from your high school)
University of Toronto undergraduate admission application
Academic transcripts (secondary school transcripts)
Personal Statement / Scholarship application form (submitted online via a personalized link after nomination)
Supporting documents requested by the university
English language proficiency certification (if applicable for U of T admission)
step_guide:
To apply for the Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship Program, follow these steps:

1.  **Get Nominated by Your High School (July - October 9, 2026):** Ask your guidance counsellor, principal, or head of school if your school is eligible to nominate students. Each school can nominate only one student. The deadline for school nominations is October 9, 2026. If your school has not participated before, they can apply to join the program by noon (EST) on October 9, 2026.
2.  **Apply for Admission to the University of Toronto (September - October 16, 2026):** Submit an application to at least one undergraduate program at the University of Toronto through the Ontario Universities' Application Centre (OUAC). You must intend to begin your studies in September 2027. The deadline to apply for admission to U of T to be considered for the Pearson Scholarship is October 16, 2026. You may apply to up to three programs, but if awarded, the scholarship would be restricted to your top choice at the time of decision.
3.  **Submit Your Pearson Scholarship Application (October - November 6, 2026):** After your school nominates you and you have submitted your University of Toronto admission application, you will receive a private, secure link to the Lester B. Pearson International Student Scholarship application. Complete this online form and submit all required documentation by November 6, 2026.
selection: Selection for the Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship is highly competitive. Candidates are chosen based on their outstanding academic achievement, creativity, and demonstrated leadership potential. Emphasis is placed on students who are identified as leaders within their schools and have the potential to contribute positively to the global community. The process begins with a nomination from the student's high school, with each school eligible to nominate only one student per year. Nominated students then complete a detailed scholarship application, showcasing their academic abilities, extracurricular engagement, and leadership qualities.
renewal: The scholarship is renewable for four years of undergraduate study, provided the scholar maintains satisfactory academic performance.

title: UBC International Major Entrance Scholarship
amount_description: The International Major Entrance Scholarship (IMES) awards are valued at CAD $10,000 – CAD $25,000 per year for the 2026/2027 academic year. This converts to approximately 675,000 INR to 1,687,500 INR annually (using an approximate exchange rate of 1 CAD = 67.5 INR). This is a partial scholarship and does not cover the full cost of tuition and living expenses, which can range from approximately CAD $68,000 to CAD $88,000 annually for international undergraduate students (tuition typically CAD $42,000-$60,000 and living expenses CAD $18,000 for two terms).
benefits:
- Be new to UBC, entering directly from secondary school or post-secondary (university or college).
- Be an international student who will be studying at UBC on a Canadian study permit (student visa). (Changes to citizenship status will affect eligibility).
- Demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and intellectual promise.
- Show impressive extracurricular and community involvement.
- Not be nominated for a need-and-merit-based International Scholars award.
- Apply to UBC by the admissions deadline for the academic year of entry.
docs_needed:
High school transcripts/academic records (or post-secondary transcripts if applicable)
Proof of English Language proficiency (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL scores)
Personal Profile (part of UBC's admission application)
Any other documents required for general UBC undergraduate admission
step_guide:
The application for the International Major Entrance Scholarship is automatic upon application for undergraduate admission to UBC. The steps for applying to UBC are:
1. **Decide on your academic program and campus:** Explore the programs offered at UBC Vancouver or UBC Okanagan.
2. **Review admission requirements:** Ensure you meet UBC's general and program-specific admission requirements, including English Language Admission Standard.
3. **Apply online:** Submit your application for undergraduate admission through the official UBC application portal. The application typically opens in early October.
4. **Submit required documents:** Follow the instructions in the 'Your UBC Application – Next Steps' email to submit all necessary supporting documents, such as transcripts and English proficiency scores. The document deadline for general admission is usually by mid-March.
5. **Meet deadlines:** Apply by the main application deadline for the Winter Session (September intake) to be automatically considered for entrance scholarships like IMES. For entry in September 2027, the application deadline is January 15, 2027.
*Note: Applications for the 2026/2027 academic year are now closed. The deadlines provided here are for the upcoming 2027/2028 academic year for which applications open in October 2026.*
selection: Eligible students are automatically considered for the International Major Entrance Scholarship (IMES) when they apply for admission to an undergraduate program at UBC by the admissions deadline; no separate scholarship application is required. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional academic achievement, intellectual promise, and impressive extracurricular and community involvement. Decisions are typically made between mid-March and the end of April. The country of origin is not a factor in the selection process.
renewal: The scholarship is renewable for up to three additional years of study, totalling four years. To maintain the scholarship, students are generally required to be registered in at least 24 courses (credits), pass all courses, and obtain a standing in the top 10 percent of their year and faculty, or an overall academic average of 75 percent or higher.

title: Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Doctoral Scholarship
amount_description: The scholarship provides an annual stipend of up to $50,000 CAD (approximately 3,372,200 INR) for three years to cover tuition and living expenses. Additionally, an annual allowance of up to $20,000 CAD (approximately 1,348,800 INR) is provided for three years to support research, networking, travel related to the doctoral project and Foundation programming, and language learning. The total annual value can be up to $70,000 CAD (approximately 4,721,500 INR). Regardless of other funding, all Scholars receive the annual $20,000 CAD allowance and a minimum of $25,000 CAD (approximately 1,686,250 INR) from the annual stipend, making the minimum total annual funding $45,000 CAD (approximately 3,035,250 INR). The total scholarship value over three years can reach up to $210,000 CAD (approximately 14,167,800 INR).
benefits:
- **Degree Level:** Exclusively for students enrolled in a PhD program. The Foundation does not provide funding for undergraduate, master's, or postdoctoral studies.
- **Year of Program:** At the time of application, candidates must be in the first or second year of a full-time doctoral program. Candidates in their final year of a master's program are no longer eligible for the 2026 Scholarship competition.
- **Field of Study:** Research must be broadly related to the humanities and social sciences, or human sciences, and be directly relevant to the future of Canada. It must also align with at least one of the Foundation's four major themes: Human Rights and Dignity; Responsible Citizenship; Canada and the World; and People and their Natural Environment.
- **Citizenship/Residency:** Candidates must be either:
  - A Canadian student studying at a Canadian university.
  - A Canadian student studying at a foreign university.
  - An international student studying at a Canadian university.
  - Note: Foreign students living outside Canada and studying at a foreign university are not eligible.
- **Commitment:** Candidates must be committed to participating in a three-year leadership program.
docs_needed:
Duly completed online application form
Transcripts from all post-secondary studies (official/final, except CEGEP in Quebec)
Four essay questions (200-400 words each)
Doctoral Project and Themes description (200-400 words, including background, objectives, theoretical framework, research methods, and references)
Personal Statement (describing what being a leader and engaged academic means and how you plan to embody these characteristics)
Information on language skills
Information on post-secondary education, achievements (e.g., scholarships, fellowships, awards, publications, conference presentations, extracurricular and volunteer activities)
Relevant work experience
Two letters of reference: at least one from an academic referee commenting on academic achievements and potential, and another that may focus on leadership abilities. Referees must complete a predetermined referral form directly on the Foundation's application portal.
step_guide:
1.  **Review Eligibility:** Carefully read all eligibility criteria on the Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation's official website to ensure you meet all requirements for the Doctoral Scholarship Program.
2.  **Register on Application Portal:** Create an account on the Foundation's online application portal. If you have an existing account from a previous competition, you may need to register a new one as the Foundation sometimes updates its portal.
3.  **Complete Application Form:** Fill out the online application completely. This includes providing details on your language skills, post-secondary education, academic achievements (including scholarships, publications, conference presentations), extracurricular activities, and relevant work experience.
4.  **Respond to Essay Questions:** Provide answers to the four essay questions (200-400 words each) and describe your doctoral project and its themes (200-400 words). Also, complete a personal statement outlining your vision of an engaged academic leader.
5.  **Upload Transcripts:** Upload official/final transcripts for all your post-secondary education directly to the application portal. Do not include CEGEP transcripts if applicable.
6.  **Request Reference Letters:** Enter the names and email addresses of your two referees in the application portal. The Foundation will send them a link to complete and submit a predetermined referral form directly. It is crucial to follow up with your referees to ensure they submit their forms before the application deadline. At least one reference must be academic, and both should address the Foundation's selection criteria, including academic excellence and leadership potential.
7.  **Submit Application:** Submit your complete application through the Foundation's online portal by the deadline (November 17, 2025, at 4:00 p.m. ET for the 2026 competition).
8.  **Inform University (Optional but Recommended):** While direct application to the Foundation is standard, it is advisable to inform your university's awards officer or Academic Unit of your intention to apply, as they may offer support or have internal processes.
selection:
The selection process is rigorous and lasts several months, involving multiple stages:
1.  **Application Review:** All eligible and complete applications submitted by the deadline are reviewed and evaluated by a selection committee.
2.  **Semi-Finalist Interviews (Round One):** Shortlisted candidates are invited for group interviews. Notifications for this round for the 2026 competition are expected in the week of January 26, 2026.
3.  **Finalist Interviews (Round Two):** Finalists are invited for individual, in-person interviews with members of the Application and Nomination Review Committee (ANRC). These final interviews for the 2026 competition are scheduled from March 10 to March 13, 2026. Note that virtual interviews will not be conducted for the 2025-2026 cycle.

**Evaluation Criteria:** Scholars are chosen based on:
*   **Academic excellence and intellectual capacity:** Demonstrated by a strong academic record, publications, and conference presentations.
*   **Research quality:** Research must be well-presented, original, and linked to the Foundation's themes.
*   **Commitment and leadership:** Exhibiting leadership qualities through initiating projects, adopting new approaches, and mobilizing knowledge.
*   **Public engagement:** A desire to contribute to public dialogue and disseminate knowledge for the benefit of others.
*   **Involvement and core values:** An interest in the Foundation's activities and events, and the ability to interact respectfully with diverse people and opinions.
renewal: The Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Doctoral Scholarship is a three-year program. Scholars must actively participate in the Foundation's learning program for the entire three-year duration. There are no possibilities for extension beyond this three-year period.

title: DAAD Research Grants for Doctoral Candidates
amount_description: The scholarship provides a monthly stipend of 1,400 EUR for doctoral candidates. Additionally, it includes an annual research allowance of 460 EUR. Payments towards health, accident, and personal liability insurance coverage are also included. A travel allowance is available upon application, and under certain circumstances, additional benefits like monthly rent subsidies and family allowances for accompanying dependents may be granted. Funding for German language courses (online or prior to the research stay) and reimbursement of TestDaF or DSH examination fees may also be available. Most German public universities generally do not charge tuition fees. The estimated annual amount in INR is calculated based on 1,400 EUR/month stipend (16,800 EUR/year) + 460 EUR/year research allowance, converted at an approximate rate of 1 EUR = 89.5 INR.
benefits:
- **Academic Qualification**: Applicants must have above-average qualifications and have completed a Master's degree, Diplom, or in exceptional cases, a Bachelor's degree, by the time the funding period begins.
- **Time Since Last Degree**: As a rule, applicants should not have graduated longer than six years before the application deadline.
- **Doctoral Status (if applicable)**: If already working on a doctoral degree, the doctorate should not have started more than three years prior to the application deadline.
- **Residency in Germany**: Applicants cannot have been resident in Germany for longer than 15 months at the application deadline.
- **Doctoral Project**: The doctoral project must be hosted by a state or state-recognized German university or a non-university research institute.
- **Supervision/Admission**: Applicants must have a confirmed German supervisor or admission to a structured doctoral program.
- **Nationality**: International students from any country are eligible to apply, provided they meet specific country requirements.
- **Language Proficiency**: Proficiency in the working language (German or English) of the doctoral project is required.
docs_needed:
Full curriculum vitae in tabular form (max. 3 pages)
List of published publications (max. 10 pages; mandatory for postdocs, highly recommended for doctoral candidates)
Letter of motivation (1-2 pages, stating academic and personal reasons for the planned research project in Germany)
Detailed research proposal (outlining objectives, methodology, significance, and a time schedule of planned research work)
Transcripts of Records (undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, showing individual grades)
All university degree certificates obtained (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, doctorate). (If the most recent degree certificate is not available at the time of application, a provisional document must be uploaded, and the official certificate submitted before the funding start date)
One recent supporting letter of recommendation (on a DAAD-form, from a university teacher, or doctoral supervisor for PhD students)
Confirmation from the academic host in Germany (must refer to the applicant's project and guarantee a workplace)
Proof of admission to doctoral studies/enrolment for doctoral studies (can be submitted at a later date if not available at the time of application)
Proof of language proficiency (German or English, depending on the working language of the project; e.g., TOEFL, IELTS, TestDaF; not older than 2 years at the application deadline)
Declaration from the scientific supervisor in Germany on safety-relevant and ethical aspects of the research project (if applicable)
Module plan (for planned participation in a structured doctoral study programme)
Proof of existing contacts to the coordinator of the doctoral programme (for structured doctoral programmes)
German or English translations of documents submitted in a national language
step_guide:
1. **Check Eligibility**: Verify that you meet all the specified nationality, academic, and language requirements.
2. **Find a Supervisor/Program**: Identify a suitable doctoral program or an academic supervisor at a state or state-recognized German university or non-university research institute and secure their confirmation/acceptance.
3. **Prepare Documents**: Gather all mandatory application documents, including your detailed research proposal, curriculum vitae, motivation letter, academic transcripts, degree certificates, and proof of language proficiency.
4. **Request Recommendation**: During the application period, generate the recommendation form from the DAAD portal and send it to your university teacher/doctoral supervisor for completion.
5. **Submit Online Application**: Access the online application form via the 'Application portal' link on the official program page (available only during the application period). Upload all required documents and submit your complete application by the deadline.
6. **Interview (if shortlisted)**: If your application is shortlisted, you may be invited for an interview, which could be conducted online.
7. **Receive Decision**: Scholarship decisions are typically communicated 3-6 months after the application deadline.
8. **Submit Certified Copies**: Successful candidates may be required to submit certified copies of their documents.
selection:
Applications are reviewed by an independent selection committee of specialist academics. The key selection criteria include:
- **Qualification**: Academic achievements, grade development, academic progress, language skills, and scholarly achievements such as publications or conference papers.
- **Research Project Quality**: Originality, topicality, relevance, choice of host institution, preparation, feasibility, and consistency of the work schedule.
- **Applicant Potential**: Career prospects, motivation for conducting research in Germany, relevant German language skills, and non-academic engagement.
Shortlisted candidates may be invited for an interview.
renewal: Funding is initially awarded for a maximum of three years. Academic achievements are assessed annually, and if satisfactory progress towards successful completion of the doctorate is demonstrated, the scholarship will continue as planned. An extension of the scholarship for a possible fourth year of funding can be applied for, allowing for a maximum funding period of four years.
